mnemonics: CLOUDS Are Distributed Systems: C - Clusters, L - Grids, O - P2P Networks, U - Ubiquitous, D - Utility, S - Autonomic
keyPoints: Distributed systems consist of multiple autonomous computers that appear as a single coherent system.
Key enabling technologies include virtualization, service-oriented architecture (SOA), and web services.
The main models are clusters, grids, peer-to-peer networks, and cloud computing which offers utility, ubiquitous, and autonomic computing.
commonMistakes: Confusing distributed systems with parallel systems; distributed systems are loosely coupled and geographically separated.
Assuming all distributed systems are the same; they differ in architecture (e.g., P2P vs. client-server) and purpose (e.g., grid vs. cloud).
Overlooking the role of middleware, which is crucial for enabling communication and management in distributed systems.
